message: Jake
Fabar
Hey, welcome
What's going on?
No, I've been having a hard time since my daughter has been ignoring my calls after we had a fight.
That sounds tough.
Totally, it's a real problem. But speaking of tough times, you've had some trouble too, right? Your suspicions have cleared up, right?
Yeah, probably, somehow.
I see. Actually, I was also questioned about it.
You too?
Yeah. In my case, they just asked for my alibi and that was it. But you really got caught in the crossfire.
Huh.
That really was tough.
Hey, you made it here.
Look

About the Director

About the serial murder case.
To another place.
Save.
Exit the game.
It really feels like a temporary home, with almost nothing here except the essentials.
This room shouldn't be occupied by anyone except the director. It's too tidy to be a man's bachelor pad.
The director probably doesn't spend many days in this room. It's almost simplistic enough to be called a hotel.
Compared to when there's a party, he looks quite lively. He's probably already thinking about the next film.
There must be some time before he starts on the next job. He seems to be truly relaxed.
With his white hair and white beard, and the wrinkles etched on his forehead, he looks like someone who's lived a life full of hardships.
Honestly, having a daughter at that age is tough. I'm thoroughly exhausted.
Saying something like that, when you don't really mean it.
No, no, I really don't know what to do.
It's the same with you, lately there's been nothing but unpleasant things happening around me.
That's so true.
I can't help but think, maybe it's some form of harassment directed at me.
Getting along well with a daughter is tough, especially girls at that age.
Starting with minor parent-child arguments, and even getting involved in my love life. She's really a troubled daughter.
Are you still traveling now?
That's right. I wonder when you'll come back.
That girl is the kind who can just wander off alone; I tried to stop her because with the recent serial murder case, it's dangerous.
But it's a good experience to travel alone.
However, thinking about the possibility of being killed...
As a parent, I think being too independent is a concern.
He doesn't even listen to what his father says; he's a troublesome kid.
Do you know where Guy traveled?
No, I haven't heard that. I heard he was planning to go on a trip, though.
Is something the matter?
Well, it’s not really something, but I was wondering when he would be back.
You don't have to worry; he’ll probably be back in time for the opening game of the major leagues.
Do you think Guy got back together with Kristy?
Since they went on a trip together, I think they should be fine, but is there something bothering you?
No, at one point I really thought it was over.
Yeah, it must have just been my imagination.
He probably didn’t want to be disturbed by anyone. You can’t be sure it won’t leak to the media if people find out.
I've been consulting with Jennifer about Patricia a lot. I feel like I'm troubling you.
Huh, troubling me?
Jennifer cried saying she was misunderstood at the party. I'm really sorry.
I didn’t think you and Jennifer were a couple.
We’re not really a couple or anything.
No, it’s okay. She’s a poor woman, so take good care of her.
If Jennifer can be happy, that’s the best thing.
It seems Meryl has been somewhere since then. She’s called, but isn’t at the hotel.
You’re worried about what happened at the party, aren’t you?
Of course. Saying it doesn’t bother me would be strange.
Right now, she’s probably calming her heart alone somewhere.
There's no way she’s thinking about suicide, right?
She's not the kind of woman who would do something like that. She's strong-willed, so at most she's just regretting not causing anyone harm.
I've caused you a lot of trouble too, huh?
Come to think of it, the first time we talked about this was at a party at my home in Boston.
Back then, I never imagined it would come to this.
It's true. Even though it's not confirmed yet, it's really distressing that the victims were people involved.
Honestly, I'd feel a lot better if it were just a simple serial murder case.
Director.
If it involves others due to personal grudges against me,
I can't help but wonder what I should do.
If you have a grudge against me, you should just target me directly.
My mood seems to have improved thanks to many of the problems that were worrying me disappearing. Concerns about my daughter are just trivial worries.
I was worried about how the filming suspension would affect the director, but it seems he doesn't care much.
I wonder if he's going to break up with Meryl.
What's with not even telling me where you're headed? You're not planning to never come back, are you?
This is the second time you've just disappeared suddenly.
The first time was an unavoidable situation, but I won't forgive you this time.
I wonder if you'll get in touch when you're in the mood.
I guess you're still worried about the events at the party. By the way, Jennifer was somehow not herself back then.
I only saw her once after the party in a hotel room. I wonder if the director knows where she is?
I wonder if Jennifer and the director have a deep relationship.
Do they still believe Guy is the killer of Cindy? It might be better to clear up the misunderstanding sooner rather than later.
Is it really over with the director? If that’s the case, Meryl would lose all her emotional support.
Trying to stab someone in front of a large crowd; there's no doubt it has left a significant scar on Meryl's career.
It’s hard to believe the killer committed murder due to a grudge against the director. Then, were the three victims just randomly chosen?
Is the killer still in New York, or have they disappeared into another city?
Could the killer be among the film industry personnel?
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
